name: Met Tran
description_short: Met Tran Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Metropolitan Transit Authority employees and families since 1950, offering checking, savings, loans, and mobile banking.
description_long:
Met Tran Federal Credit Union was chartered by the National Credit Union Administration in 1950 and has been operating for 70 years. It serves over 2,600 members who are employees and family members of the Metropolitan Transit Authority of Harris County in Texas. The credit union operates a single branch location at 1900 Main Street in Houston and maintains a 24-hour ATM for member convenience.

The institution offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, mobile and online banking with real-time account access, direct deposit setup, online bill pay, money orders, and financial counseling. They provide loan products with recent emphasis on auto refinancing (up to 1% rate reduction advertised), truck, and boat loans. The credit union actively promotes membership growth with current promotions on new accounts and dividend rate increases.

Met Tran FCU distinguishes itself through its employee-specific membership model and cooperative structure emphasizing member partnerships and financial goal achievement. The organization highlights its 70-year history of service and community-focused approach with slogans like "People Helping People." As a federal credit union, it operates under NCUA oversight and regulatory protections.

Met Tran is best suited for current and former Metropolitan Transit Authority employees and their immediate family members given its membership restrictions. The institution is relatively small with limited branch infrastructure (one physical location), which may not accommodate those needing widespread branch access. However, mobile banking and the 24-hour ATM offset some convenience limitations for existing members. The advertised loan refinancing promotions and recent dividend rate increases suggest competitive positioning within its membership niche. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Federally chartered credit union with NCUA insurance protections for member deposits
24-hour ATM available at credit union for convenient cash access outside business hours
Mobile banking platform providing real-time account access and control via phone
Auto loan refinancing with up to 1% rate reduction prominently advertised
Recently raised dividend rates on savings products
Financial counseling services offered to members at no cost
cons: Single physical branch location (1900 Main Street, Houston) severely limits in-person service accessibility
Restricted membership limited to Metropolitan Transit Authority employees and family members only
Limited business hours (Monday-Friday 9am-4pm) with no weekend services
Small membership base (2,600 members) may indicate limited product development resources compared to larger institutions
No information provided regarding loan approval rates, credit requirements, or competitive APR rates
best_for: Current and former Metropolitan Transit Authority employees seeking basic banking services
Families of MTHA employees looking for member-owned financial institution benefits
Members wanting to refinance existing auto, truck, or boat loans
Houston-area residents with existing membership seeking mobile-first banking convenience
diagnosis: Met Tran FCU is best for current or retired Metropolitan Transit Authority employees and their families seeking straightforward banking services from a member-owned institution. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is strictly limited to MTHA employees and relatives, making this institution inaccessible to the general public, and the single branch location in Houston requires local access or reliance on mobile banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
